title: 한국에는 사실상 아무도 없다는 MBTI 성격유형 TOP3
text: Hello everyone, this is King's Cream. The MBTI is the most widely known personality test currently in existence, and it has been steadily gaining popularity in Korea for several years now. According to the Korea Psychological Testing Institute, the ISTJ type accounts for the largest proportion of Koreans, comprising approximately 25% of the total population. It was revealed that ESTJ ranks second with 15%, and IST and D each take third place with 5%. However, on the other hand, there are MBTI types that boast such extreme low percentages that they are difficult to find among Koreans. Today, shall we take a look at the top 3 MBTI personalities that are virtually non-existent in Korea? 3. ENTP: A 21st-century talent. There is a personality type that is accompanied by good news such as being a jack-of-all-trades. It is the NTP, the argumentative type who enjoys heated debates. Due to their unique personality of liking discussions and seeking the attention of others, they often become the stars of countless memes overseas. However, in South Korea, only 2% belong to this type. According to the Korea Psychological Testing Institute, the proportion of ENTPs in the global population is around 3.2%. Even on a global scale, it is rare. Although it is a matter of level, the proportion of ENTPs in Korea is only 5% to 2%, which can be seen as being particularly lower only in South Korea. Regarding this phenomenon, some argue that it is a result of the harmful effects of rote learning. ENTPs possess a strong temperament that desires to bring their own ideas and concepts into reality rather than merely studying theory; however, the Korean education system, which is preoccupied with teaching theory, lacks the capacity to cultivate talent suitable for ENTPs, and therefore, the number of people with this type in Korea is inevitably so low. Upon hearing this, it is quite convincing, and to support this claim, it is said that the number of ENTPs in Korea has been steadily increasing recently due to the continuous reform of the education curriculum. I hope the day comes soon when ENTPs can fully unleash their abilities in South Korea. Coming in at 2nd place is the ENTJ. The personality type that is said to be the most

title: 한국에는 사실상 아무도 없다는 MBTI 성격유형 TOP3
text: g recently due to the continuous reform of the education curriculum. I hope the day comes soon when ENTPs can fully unleash their abilities in South Korea. Coming in at 2nd place is the ENTJ. The personality type that is said to be the most common in South Korea, accounting for only about 1,008% of the population, is the ENTJ. Bold and assertive, ENTJs are the most socially successful personality type, despite their small numbers. Based on their leadership and command, they [manage] society It is known that they are active across all walks of life. Although less than 2% of Koreans fall into the ENTJ type, if you look at just the last two digits, the PJ type actually makes up a very large portion of the Korean public. In reality, the personality types that make up the largest proportion in Korea are IST, YS, and DJ. The most persuasive argument is that the reason there are so few people belonging to the EMP type, even though I belong to the PJ type like myself, is due to Korea's unique culture. Since this type is fundamentally rational, logical, and tends to prefer a systematic environment, most Korean companies are the most preferred talents, and they pray with the 4Ps in mind. However, given the atmosphere in Korean society that suppresses creativity and prevents standing out, the transition to pursuing an open organizational culture is bound to occur in Korean society. As these individuals have adjusted their innate personalities to align with what society prefers, the number of ENTJs among the P&G types has inevitably decreased. The argument is that ENTJs are jokingly called the type that resists "Kkondae" the most. If any of you watching this video belong to the ENTJ type, for the bright future of Korea, [we will continue to...] I will be rooting for you to continue developing your personality. 1st Place: ENFJ. This personality type is said to be possessed by the smallest number of people in the Korean population— specifically, the righteous social activist type, the ENFJ. ENFJs make up only 2% of the Korean population, and it is even known that the ratio among Korean men is around 0.5%. Therefore, it is not an exaggeration to say that they are practically non-existent. The reason the proportion of ENFJs among Koreans is so exceptionally low is that the characteristics of the 2 N F G (Natural, Artificial, and G) often clash with societal demands. ENFJs are

title: 한국에는 사실상 아무도 없다는 MBTI 성격유형 TOP3
text: ion to say that they are practically non-existent. The reason the proportion of ENFJs among Koreans is so exceptionally low is that the characteristics of the 2 N F G (Natural, Artificial, and G) often clash with societal demands. ENFJs are so emotional and sociable that they are sometimes called "human puppies" who love people too much, and they are known for their immediate consideration of others. However, when this gentle personality—which involves trusting others without suspicion and finding it difficult to refuse requests— clashes with the harsh reality, they often end up becoming "gullible customers" (suckers) and frequently suffer psychological trauma, such as falling victim to voice phishing scams. It is said that most people exhibiting this type often transform into a different type as they become adults and gain experience in social life. Due to the wounds received from society, their warm personality... It feels somewhat bittersweet to see things changing. To all of you Youngsoons belonging to the ENFJ type, I hope you can walk a path worthy of being ranked without getting hurt in the future. Ranking School will be cheering for you. MBTI is nothing more than classifying the tens of millions of personalities that exist in the world into just 16 categories. No personality test can perfectly explain us. Therefore, I hope you understand that the personalities of everyone in the world are special and precious, not just the four types we introduced. This has been Ranking School. Thank you. [Music]
